title: reseda lutea subsp. neglecta
intro: Reseda lutea subsp. neglecta, also known as the dill weed or wild dill, is a subspecies of the yellow reseda plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Reseda lutea subsp. neglecta include reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and providing relief from digestive issues such as diarrhea and nausea. Herbal preparations of the plant often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
